ready/_listenForTermination -> end pipeline. Each plugin lazily dynamic-imports serverless-offline/lambda (ESM-only in v13) to instantiate the Lambda runner, merges options through a fixed five-source precedence chain, extracts the relevant event type from each function definition (resolving CloudFormation Fn::GetAtt ARNs to concrete queue/stream/table names), and registers SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that call end(), which cleans up the runner and the service then calls process.exit(0). Behavior is gated for tests via NODE_ENV==='test' and an end(skipExit) escape hatch. The code is essentially copy-pasted across packages, with several latent bugs (this.region never set, log.warn vs log.warning, kinesis/dynamodb service files missing the log import, redundant null-then-assign field initialization, and an unused skipStart-only _create code path in s3.js).","gotchas":["Redundant field init: the constructor sets every field to null and then immediately reassigns cliOptions and serverless (index.js:21-28). Same pattern in the service classes (e.g. s3/s3.js:16-22, sqs/sqs.js:26-32, kinesis/kinesis.js:14-19).","this.region is read but NEVER assigned. NEW src/log.js: the canonical console-fallback log shim ({defaultLog, normalizeLog}). It is the canonical expression `log => ({...defaultLog, ...(log || {})})` byte-identical to the goal/plan spec, kept on a line carrying an inline `eslint-disable-next-line unicorn/no-useless-fallback-in-spread` directive (see issuesOrDeferred) so the explicit {} fallback survives the lint gate while staying verbatim to the spec.

src/index.js: removed `require('@serverless/utils/log')`; added `const {normalizeLog} = require('./log')`. Constructor is now `constructor(serverless, cliOptions, {log} = {})` setting `this.log = normalizeLog(log)`; dropped the redundant declare-null pre-assignments (per the plan's explicit house-cleanup instruction, which overrides the general declare-null idiom for this file). Replaced all three `this.serverless.cli.log(...)` calls (start banner, _listenForTermination, end) with `this.log.notice(...)`. `_mergeOptions` debug log is now `this.log.debug('s3 options:', this.options)`. `_createS3` threads the logger: `new S3(this.lambda, resources, this.options, this.log)`. Kept the dynamic `await import('serverless-offline/lambda')`.

src/s3.js: removed `require('@serverless/utils/log')` and the now-unused `require('./s3-event-definition')`. Constructor is now `constructor(lambda, resources, options, log)` with `this.log = normalizeLog(log)`. Deleted the dead `_create` and `_s3Event` methods (the latter called `listener.stop()` immediately after registering — orphaned). The live `start()` catch now uses `this.log.warning(err.stack)` (was the no-op `log.warn`).

package.json: added `src/log.js` to `files`; bumped version 7.0.0 -> 7.1.0 (minor, back-compatible). Peer kept at `^10.0.2 || >=11`.

README.md: added a `## Serverless Framework v4` note (works on v3 and v4; logging now uses the framework-injected logger with console fallback).

NEW test/index.js (AVA, docker-free): unit tests for normalizeLog (all levels present; merges a partial {notice}; defaults for missing levels; normalizeLog(undefined)/normalizeLog(null) safe; default debug is a quiet noop; does not mutate the injected logger), the pure S3Event mapper (wraps a representative Minio S3 record into the AWS Lambda event shape — Records[0].s3.bucket.name, object.key, eventSource 'aws:s3', awsRegion; preserves record reference), and source-level regression guards (no `@serverless/utils/log` / `serverless.cli.log`, uses log.warning not log.warn, dead _create/_s3Event and the s3-event-definition require are gone, constructor takes the 3rd-arg {log}). <result>{"rawFindings":15,"deduped":15,"confirmedCount":8,"confirmed":[{"title":"#211 queueName override is silently ignored for the common (object-with-arn) event shapes","file":"packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js:64-70 (resolveQueueName -> SQSEventDefinition), packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs-event-definition.js:13-30","severity":"major","fix":"Make the override authoritative regardless of how queueName would otherwise be derived. Minimal fix in resolveQueueName (packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js:31-34): strip `arn` when applying the override so SQSEventDefinition's switch falls through to the queueName branch (which rebuilds the arn from the new queueName at sqs-event-definition.js:34). lodash/fp omit is already imported in sqs-event-definition.js; import it (or `pick`/spread) in sqs.js too:

const resolveQueueName = (options, rawSqsEventDefinition) =>
  options && options.queueName
    ? typeof rawSqsEventDefinition === 'string'
      ? {queueName: options.queueName}
      : {...omit(['arn'], rawSqsEventDefinition), queueName: options.queueName}
    : rawSqsEventDefinition;

Verified at runtime: with this change all 5 README forms resolve queueName='override' and arn='arn:aws:sqs:eu-west-1:<acct>:override', and other props (e.g. batchSize) are preserved. This also keeps SQSEvent's eventSourceARN (sqs.js:130) consistent with the overridden local queue. Add a regression test for an {arn:'arn:...'} def with options.queueName set (and ideally one for {queueName,arn} form3), asserting sqsEvent.queueName==='override' and sqsEvent.arn ends with ':override' — the existing test/index.js:176-181 misses this because its def has no arn.","rationale":"Confirmed by reading the code and reproducing at runtime. resolveQueueName (packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js:31-34) correctly returns {...rawDef, queueName: override} and is wired into _create (sqs.js:65-67). But the resulting def is passed to SQSEventDefinition, whose switch (packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs-event-definition.js:13-30) evaluates `case typeof rawSqsEventDefinition.arn` (line 19) BEFORE `case typeof rawSqsEventDefinition.queueName` (line 24). Since switch runs the first strictly-equal case and break-stops, any def carrying a string `arn` takes the arn branch (line 20) and overwrites queueName from the ARN, discarding the override.

Runtime reproduction (resolveQueueName + SQSEventDefinition) with options.queueName='override':
- form1 bare string 'arn:...:MyFirstQueue' -> queueName 'override' (override applied)
- form2 {arn:'...'} -> 'MySecondQueue' (override IGNORED)
- form3 {queueName,arn:'...'} -> 'MyThirdQueue' (IGNORED)
- form4/5 {arn:'...'} (Fn::GetAtt) -> 'MyFourthQueue' (IGNORED)

I also verified packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/index.js:201-214 (_resolveFn) converts Fn::GetAtt into a string `arn` before the event reaches SQS.create, so README forms 4/5 really do arrive as {arn:'arn:...'} strings — confirming the override is broken for 4 of the 5 documented shapes (README.md:59-77), and only the bare-string form works. The unit test at test/index.js:176-181 only exercises a def with queueName and NO arn, giving false confidence. README.md:114 promises the override applies to "each sqs event definition", which is false for the realistic arn-bearing cases. This is a re-implemented community PR feature (#211) that is broadly non-functional, hence major."},{"title":"#211 resolveQueueName spreads a string ARN into a char-indexed object","file":"packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js:31-34","severity":"minor","fix":"In packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js, normalize string input before spreading and drop arn so the override wins:

const resolveQueueName = (options, rawSqsEventDefinition) => {
  if (!(options && options.queueName)) return rawSqsEventDefinition;
  const base =
    typeof rawSqsEventDefinition === 'string'
      ? {arn: rawSqsEventDefinition}
      : rawSqsEventDefinition;
  return {...omit(['arn'], base), queueName: options.queueName};
};

(omit is already imported from lodash/fp in sqs.js.) This yields a clean def object (no char-indexed keys) and makes the override actually win for both the string-ARN and object-with-arn forms, since stripping arn forces SQSEventDefinition's switch to use the .queueName branch. Add a test asserting the resolved def has no numeric keys (e.g. t.deepEqual(Object.keys(resolved).sort(), ['queueName'])) and that a constructed SQSEventDefinition has no stray numeric keys, to lock the regression.","rationale":"CONFIRMED by reproduction. In packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js:31-34, resolveQueueName does `{...rawSqsEventDefinition, queueName: options.queueName}`. When rawSqsEventDefinition is a string ARN (README form 1) AND an override is set, the spread iterates the string character-by-character, producing `{0:'a',1:'r',2:'n',...,'43':..., queueName:'override'}` (object with .arn === undefined). I ran the real code: resolveQueueName returns an object whose first 10 keys are '0'..'9'. It "works" only because SQSEventDefinition's switch (sqs-event-definition.js:13-30) checks `typeof rawDef.arn` (undefined → string-typeof miss) and falls through to the `.queueName` branch. Then `Object.assign(this, omit(['arn','queueName','enabled'], def))` at sqs-event-definition.js:38 copies all 44 numeric char keys onto the instance — the resulting SQSEventDefinition has 47 keys ('0'..'43','enabled','arn','queueName'). The test at test/index.js:127-131 only asserts resolved.queueName === 'override', which masks the malformation. The stray keys are currently inert because downstream _sqsEvent (sqs.js:96) only destructures named fields, so this is a data-hygiene/correctness defect, not a runtime crash — hence minor. Note: my reproduction also confirmed the related issue that for the object-with-arn form, the override is silently ignored because the switch prefers the .arn branch over .queueName; stripping arn in the override path (as the proposed fix does) fixes that too."},{"title":"Orphaned s3-event-definition.js remains in source and in the published files list","severity":"nit","fix":"Optional leftover cleanup (severity nit): delete the now-orphaned module packages/serverless-offline-s3/src/s3-event-definition.js, and remove its entry "src/s3-event-definition.js" (line 19) from the `files` array in packages/serverless-offline-s3/package.json. Do not modify the kinesis/sqs/dynamodb-streams *-event-definition.js files, which are still imported and live. The negative test assertion in test/index.js:129 needs no change (it asserts absence of the import, which remains true).","rationale":"Verified and accurate. The PR removed the `require('./s3-event-definition')` import from packages/serverless-offline-s3/src/s3.js (confirmed in the staged diff) and deleted the only two consumers of S3EventDefinition, the `_create` and `_s3Event` methods. I confirmed there are no remaining live references to the module: `grep -rn s3-event-definition packages/serverless-offline-s3/` returns only (a) package.json:19, where the file is still listed in the published `files` array, and (b) test/index.js:129, which is a NEGATIVE assertion `t.false(source.includes("require('./s3-event-definition')"))`, not an actual import. The file packages/serverless-offline-s3/src/s3-event-definition.js (the S3EventDefinition class) still exists, untouched (dated Mar 26 2024), and is now genuinely dead/orphaned code that still ships in the npm package.
